title: nepeta woodiana
intro: Nepeta woodiana, also known as Wood's catmint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving anxiety and insomnia, reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting respiratory health. The leaves and flowers of Nepeta woodiana can be used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal remedies.
